problems.title: Grade Assigner
problems.statement: Convert a numeric score into a letter grade: 90+ is A, 80–89 is B, 70–79 is C, 60–69 is D, below 60 is F.
problems.starterCode:
score = int(input())
# print the letter grade

problems.title: Second Largest
problems.statement: Given a list of distinct integers, find the second-largest value.
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
# print the second-largest value

problems.title: Word Frequency
problems.statement: Given a sentence, find the word that appears most often. You can assume there's a single clear winner.

examples.input: the cat sat on the mat the cat ran

constraints: Words are lowercase and separated by single spaces.
problems.starterCode:
sentence = input()
# print the most frequent word

stdin: the cat sat on the mat the cat ran
a b c a b a
quick brown fox jumps over lazy dog quick

problems.title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
problems.statement: Given an n×n grid of integers, sum the numbers on the main diagonal (top-left to bottom-right).
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
# print the diagonal sum

problems.title: Digital Root
problems.statement: Given a positive integer, repeatedly add its digits together until only one digit remains. Print that digit.
constraints: The input is a positive integer.
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
# repeatedly sum the digits until one digit remains, then print it

problems.title: Robot on a Grid
problems.statement: A robot starts at (0, 0) on a grid and receives a string of movement commands: N (north, y+1), S (south, y-1), E (east, x+1), W (west, x-1). Print its final position as two integers, x then y, separated by a space.

examples.note: 3 N's and 1 S give y=2; one E and one W cancel out, so x=0

constraints: The command string contains only the letters N, S, E, W.
problems.starterCode:
commands = input()
x, y = 0, 0
# walk through each command and update x, y
# then print(x, y) in the form "x y"

problems.title: Mountain Peak
problems.statement: Given a list of numbers, find every "peak": a value that is strictly greater than each of its immediate neighbors. The first and last elements only have one neighbor, so they count as peaks if they're greater than that single neighbor. Print the peaks, in order, space-separated.
problems.starterCode:
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
# find and print the peaks, space-separated

problems.title: Election Winner
problems.statement: Count votes (names separated by commas, possibly with extra spaces) and print the winner in the exact form Winner:name (no space after the colon).

examples.input: Hari, Hari, Yadava, Yadava, Madhava, Hari, Hari

constraints: Assume there's a single clear winner (no ties).
problems.starterCode:
line = input()
names = [n.strip() for n in line.split(",")]
# count votes and print "Winner:name"

stdin: Hari, Hari, Yadava, Yadava, Madhava, Hari, Hari
